titleStmt.title: Letter from Helen Sophia Chenevix to Hanna Sheehy-Skeffington, 10 December 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Helen Sophia Chenevix

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Helen Sophia Chenevix (1890–1963) was an Irish campaigner for women's suffrage and a trade unionist. Hanna Sheehy-Skeffington was also a suffragette and concerned about social reform.

msIdentifier.collection: Sheehy Skeffington Papers, Collection List No. 47; Ms 33,612/14-1

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Office—29 South Anne Street.
Dublin. 10th Dec 1916 Dear Mrs Skeffington, Many thanks for you p.c. We discussed the question
of sending a representative to your meeting about the
government Registration Bill at new Committee yesterday,
and felt that in the whol. it was not quite the
moment for such a meeting, in view of the fact that the
bill seems likely to be withdrawn, or so watered down
that the franchise will not be attend in any way. Yours sincerely, Ms. Flaherty.
